title: ED fracture suspicion documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

assessment.text: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

assessment.text: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.
assessment.rationale: The exact NICE non-complex-fracture assessment section supports mechanism, onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and manual focused assessment without confirming a fracture.

source.title: Fractures (non-complex): assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Children and adults with non-complex fractures managed in emergency or orthopaedic services.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected non-complex fracture assessment, documentation, imaging, weight-bearing, information, and follow-up contexts; partial for a generic fracture-follow-up workflow and not validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

assessment.text: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC extremity-trauma section supports recording vital data, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only when assessed; it does not auto-state neurovascular status.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured
source.population: Adults and children with acute illness or injury presenting to first-contact emergency care.
source.setting: First-contact emergency units and resource-limited emergency-care settings.
source.exclusions: Exact for structured trauma history, secondary survey, extremity perfusion, and open-wound assessment; partial for minor outpatient injury follow-up, imaging, closure, antibiotics, and UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

assessment.text: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact documentation and information section supports recording existing imaging, clinician plan, completed discussion and arranged handover only as clinician-confirmed facts.

source.title: Fractures (non-complex): assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Children and adults with non-complex fractures managed in emergency or orthopaedic services.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected non-complex fracture assessment, documentation, imaging, weight-bearing, information, and follow-up contexts; partial for a generic fracture-follow-up workflow and not validated for UAE pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

assessment.text: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.
assessment.rationale: Management recommendations were reviewed only to delimit clinician-documented actions; no immobilisation, analgesia, manipulation or referral is generated.

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
